summary: Iteration 3 testing complete. Backend: 46/46 pytest pass in ~45s (27 previous + 19 new covering stream-token issuance/rejection, subscription me/upgrade/downgrade, premium gating for socratic/motivational, bridge mode 402/400/200 bilingual output, intervention TTL cache, free lesson quota enforcement, admin analytics plan_distribution + intervention/bridge buckets). Frontend: /upgrade page, navbar Upgrade/Premium pills, student dashboard locks+banner, Bridge modal and teacher interventions gating all verified end-to-end on the real preview URL. Fixed 2 critical frontend bugs blocking the whole free-student experience (see backend_issues/frontend_issues).

minor.issue: await db.users.update_many({plan: {$exists: false}}, {$set: {plan: 'free'}}) downgrades any seeded admin/teacher users that pre-existed from iter1/iter2 (before plan field) to 'free'. Expected seeded admin+teacher to be PREMIUM per spec. Tests self-heal by auto-upgrading via /api/subscription/upgrade but on a fresh deploy with old DB data the seed assumption breaks.

ui_bugs.issue: FIXED by testing agent — useApp() only destructured {user, lang}; missing isPremium and subscription caused ReferenceError: 'isPremium is not defined' crashing the whole student dashboard on render (red runtime-error overlay). Fixed to destructure { user, lang, isPremium, subscription }.

FIXED by testing agent — AVATARS entries for 'socratic' and 'motivational' were missing premium:true flag, so locked = a.premium && !isPremium was always false; Lock icon + 'Pro' pill never rendered for free users, and clicking them did NOT redirect to /upgrade. Added premium:true to both entries.

action_items: Consider tightening the lifespan plan-backfill so seeded admin/teacher are explicitly re-asserted as premium on startup (e.g. update_one({email: 'admin@lms.com'}, {$set: {plan: 'premium'}}) idempotent), instead of defaulting to free.

The two frontend fixes applied by testing agent (destructure isPremium/subscription; premium:true on socratic/motivational) should be reviewed and kept — they were genuine bugs hiding behind an uncaught ReferenceError.
critical_code_review_comments: StudentDashboard crashed for ALL free users due to isPremium reference — this would have fully broken the free-tier UX in production. Add an error boundary around dashboards so one missing prop doesn't whitescreen the page.

Premium-gate config is split between backend (PREMIUM_PERSONALITIES set) and frontend (AVATARS[i].premium). These drifted (frontend missed the flag). Centralise the personality metadata OR return `premium_personalities` from /api/subscription/me so frontend never hardcodes it.

lifespan plan backfill sets everyone without a plan to 'free' — this is fine for real users but flattens seeded premium accounts created pre-iter3. Safer: seed by email on every startup with idempotent $setOnInsert / explicit role→plan mapping.
/api/auth/stream-token lives on current_user which itself now refuses kind='stream' — that's correct. Good defence-in-depth: a stolen stream token can't mint another stream token.

Intervention TTL cache is an in-memory dict with no size cap — on a multi-worker gunicorn this becomes inconsistent per-worker AND leaks memory over time. Use a TTL LRU (cachetools.TTLCache) and Redis for multi-worker.
/api/subscription/upgrade has no idempotency key, no audit log, no payment — safe for demo but be loud about it in logs when toggled (consider logger.warning so ops sees every 'upgrade').
Free lesson quota uses `ai_usage` kind='lesson' count which also increments on non-student users; for students only this is fine but the query doesn't filter by role — harmless today, brittle later.
Bridge endpoint trusts the model to produce valid JSON — if Groq returns prose it 500s. Consider a safe_json_parse fallback that retries once with a stricter reminder.
test_credentials: admin@lms.com/admin123, teacher@lms.com/teacher123, student@lms.com/student123 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md). Seeded student reset to free at end of test run.
seed_data_creation: Fixture-created TEST_sub_* (subscription upgrade/downgrade), TEST_quota_* (lesson quota), TEST_lsn_* (fresh-student lessons) and some TEST_curr curriculum items. No destructive changes to seed data; seeded student downgraded back to 'free' after frontend tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Stream endpoint /api/tutor/chat/stream now REQUIRES kind='stream' token — call POST /api/auth/stream-token first with normal JWT, use the returned stream_token in ?token= query. Chat input on lesson page has data-testid='chat-input'. Seeded admin+teacher may come up as plan='free' if the DB is pre-iter3 — run POST /api/subscription/upgrade for them (as the tokens fixture now does automatically). Iter3 bridge modal takes ~6-10s (Groq streaming JSON) — wait 12s after clicking chat-bridge.
rca_of_the_issue: Two frontend bugs both stemmed from incomplete wiring of the new subscription state into StudentDashboard: (1) useApp() destructure missed isPremium/subscription causing a ReferenceError that React rendered as a full-page runtime-error overlay — nothing on /student worked; (2) AVATARS config never got the premium:true flag on socratic/motivational, so the gating check `a.premium && !isPremium` was always false. Both fixed in place; tests now pass on real preview URL.
